w:r.w:t: Dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein | Dr EA Kamalie Dental Surgery
Visit Dr Emile Kamalie, a trusted Dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein, offering general dentistry, orthodontics, cosmetic treatments, and family dentistry.
Modern Dental Care in Strandfontein and Muizenberg
Visit a trusted dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein, Dr Emile Kamalie, who offers general dentistry, orthodontics, cosmetic treatments, and gentle dental care for children.
Community-Focused Dental Care For The Whole Family
Dr Emile Kamalie, a dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein, provides comprehensive dental care for patients of all ages.

From routine check-ups and restorative dentistry to orthodontic and aesthetic treatments, we provide various dental treatments. Our goal is simple: we aim to help you maintain healthy teeth and feel confident about your smile.
Children, teens, and adults are all welcome. Sedation options are also available for patients who feel anxious about dental visits or for younger patients who may need extra comfort during treatment.

Dr Emile Ambrose Kamalie is a qualified general dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein who also provides orthodontics and cosmetic dentistry. He works with patients of all ages and aims to provide comfortable dental care for anxious patients or children through sedation options.
Outside of dentistry, Dr Kamalie is very much a family man. When he is not in his dental practice, he is a husband and father to three girls, including one very special pitbull.

As a dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein, Dr Emile Kamalie provides routine dental care such as examinations, cleanings, fillings, crowns, root canal treatment, and extractions to help maintain long-term oral health.
Repairing damaged or missing teeth using treatments such as fillings, crowns, bridges, and other procedures to restore function and comfort to your smile.
Cosmetic treatments, including composite bonding, composite veneers, and injection moulding, are available from a dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein to enhance the appearance of your smile.
Clear aligners and orthodontic treatments offered by a dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein help straighten teeth and improve bite alignment for both teenagers and adults.
Children receive gentle and supportive care with dental treatments designed to protect and guide growing smiles.
Endodontic treatment focuses on saving teeth affected by infection or deep decay, with procedures such as root canal therapy performed by our dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein.
See What Our Patients Say About Us
Trusted dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein offering comprehensive dental care for adults and children.
1 Capri Way, San Remo, Strandfontein, Cape Town, 7798
Dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein | About | Dr EA Kamalie Dental Surgery
Meet Dr Emile Kamalie, a dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein offering comprehensive dental care for adults and children. Contact us for more information.

Dr Emile Ambrose Kamalie is a qualified dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein who obtained his Bachelor of Dental Surgery from the University of the Western Cape. He works with patients of all ages and offers sedation for children to make dental visits more comfortable.
Dr Kamalie opened his practice in Strandfontein at the age of 27 and has recently expanded his services to Muizenberg, allowing him to care for more patients across the community.

Outside of the practice, Dr Kamalie has a few interests that patients often enjoy hearing about. He is a dedicated Arsenal supporter, takes pride in his sneaker collection, and keeps up with the latest anime and manga. He also enjoys spending time around the braai with family and friends.
Above all, Dr Kamalie is family-oriented. When he is not working, he is a husband and a father to three girls, including one very special pitbull.
Dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein | General Dentistry | Dr EA Kamalie Dental Surgery
Visit a Dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein for general dentistry, including exams, cleanings, extractions, X-rays, and routine care for healthy teeth.

A healthy smile rarely happens by chance. It is built through small habits, regular care, and attention to changes in your teeth and gums. When something feels different, it helps to have a dentist who can assess the situation early and guide you through the next steps.

Dr Emile Kamalie provides general dental care for patients of all ages at his practices. As a dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein, he focuses on everyday treatments that keep your mouth healthy and functioning comfortably.
What happens during a general dental visit?

Your appointment usually starts with a conversation about how your teeth and gums have been feeling. You may mention sensitivity when drinking something cold, getting food stuck between certain teeth, or a tooth that has started to feel uncomfortable.

A clinical examination follows, where Dr Kamalie looks closely at your teeth, gums, and bite. When needed, intra-oral x-rays are taken to reveal areas that cannot be seen during a visual examination, such as early decay between teeth or changes around the roots. These images help identify problems before they become more complicated. Early detection often means simpler treatment.

Even if you brush carefully every day, plaque and hardened deposits can still accumulate over time. A professional scale and polish removes these deposits and helps reduce the risk of gum irritation or
During a cleaning appointment, the following steps may take place:
Removal of plaque and hardened tartar from the teeth
Polishing to smooth surfaces and remove surface stains
Guidance on brushing and flossing habits that support daily care
These appointments are also a good opportunity to discuss concerns about your smile. Some patients later explore treatments such as
or other aesthetic options after maintaining their oral health through regular cleanings.
Dr Kamalie aims to preserve your natural teeth wherever possible. However, there are situations where extractions are recommended to protect your overall oral health.
A tooth may need to be removed when:
Severe decay has weakened the tooth structure
A tooth is crowding surrounding teeth or affecting alignment
If you are considering treatments that improve tooth positioning, such as
therapy, removing problematic teeth may sometimes create the space needed for teeth to move into healthier positions.
What if I feel nervous about dental treatment?

Feeling uneasy about dental visits is more common than many people realise. Some patients have had uncomfortable experiences in the past. Others simply feel anxious about dental instruments or procedures.

To help create a more manageable experience, conscious sedation may be offered in certain situations. This approach allows you to remain relaxed and calm during treatment while still being responsive. Sedation can also be helpful for younger patients who may struggle to sit through a procedure comfortably.
How does general dentistry support my long-term smile?

General dentistry does more than treat problems. It helps maintain the health of your teeth so that other treatments, such as orthodontic care or cosmetic improvements, can be considered when appropriate.
Routine examinations, professional cleanings, and timely treatment all play a role in protecting your natural teeth.
If you are looking for a dentist in Muizenberg or Strandfontein, you can
an appointment with Dr Emile Kamalie to discuss your dental needs and take the next step toward maintaining a healthy smile.
Dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein | Restorative Dentistry | Dr EA Kamalie Dental Surgery
Restore damaged or missing teeth with a Dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein offering fillings, crowns, and bridges to support everyday function. Book now.

A chipped tooth, a cavity that has started to cause discomfort, or a gap where a tooth used to be are more common than many people realise. They can also affect how you eat, speak, and feel about your smile. Restorative dentistry offered by our dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein focuses on repairing teeth that have been damaged or replacing teeth that are missing.
Restorative dentistry includes treatments that rebuild or replace teeth that have been affected by decay, fractures, or wear.
You might need restorative care if you notice:
A tooth that has cracked or chipped
A missing tooth that affects chewing

These problems can sometimes begin quietly. A small cavity may not cause pain immediately. That is why routine visits and a scale and polish appointment often play an important role in identifying issues early.
When a concern is found, restorative treatment focuses on repairing the tooth in a way that allows you to continue using it comfortably.
How do fillings repair a tooth?
Fillings are commonly used when a cavity forms in the tooth. The damaged portion of the tooth is removed, and the space is restored with a durable filling material.
Restore the natural shape of the tooth
Allow you to chew comfortably again

When treated early, a filling can be a simple solution that prevents the need for more complex procedures later. During your appointment, Dr Kamalie may also check the surrounding teeth to ensure that no additional areas require attention.

Sometimes a tooth becomes too weakened to support a filling alone. This may happen after extensive decay or a fracture that affects a larger portion of the tooth. In these situations, a crown may be recommended.

A crown covers the visible part of the tooth and helps restore its strength and structure. It can allow you to continue using the tooth without discomfort while protecting what remains of the natural tooth underneath.
Crowns are also sometimes placed after certain treatments involving the inner part of the tooth, which may be addressed during
What if a tooth is missing?
When a tooth is lost, the surrounding teeth may begin to shift over time. This can affect how your bite feels and how evenly pressure is distributed when chewing.
A bridge is one way to replace a missing tooth. It works by anchoring a replacement tooth to the neighbouring teeth, filling the gap and restoring function.
For some patients, restoring a missing tooth also creates a stronger foundation if they later consider treatments that improve the alignment or appearance of their smile, such as
How can restorative dentistry restore my smile?
Restorative dentistry focuses on helping you keep your teeth working as they should. When teeth function comfortably, everyday activities like eating, speaking, and smiling feel easier.
If you have noticed discomfort, damage, or a missing tooth, it may be time to explore restorative treatment with a dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein.
an appointment with Dr Emile Kamalie to discuss your concerns and learn which restorative options may be appropriate for your smile.
Dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein | Cosmetic Dentistry | Dr EA Kamalie Dental Surgery
Explore cosmetic dentistry with a Dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein offering composite bonding, veneers, and smile enhancements. Book a consultation.

Your smile is often one of the first things people notice about you. Small details, such as a chipped edge, uneven spacing, or worn enamel, can slowly affect the way you feel about your teeth. Cosmetic dentistry focuses on improving the appearance of your smile while still respecting the natural structure of your teeth.
Dr Kamalie offers cosmetic dental treatments at his practices as a dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein, helping patients make subtle changes that can refresh the look of their smiles.

Cosmetic dentistry focuses on improving the appearance of teeth while keeping your smile natural and balanced. Treatment plans often start with a conversation about what you notice when you look in the mirror.
Teeth that appear uneven in shape
Discolouration that does not respond to routine cleaning
Before cosmetic work begins, Dr Kamalie may first check the health of your teeth during a
or cleaning appointment. Maintaining healthy gums and teeth creates a stable foundation for aesthetic treatments.
How does composite bonding improve my smile?
Composite bonding is often used to reshape or rebuild small areas of a tooth. A tooth-coloured material is carefully placed and shaped to blend with the surrounding enamel.
Areas where enamel has worn down

Because the material is shaped directly on the tooth, adjustments can often be made in a single visit. Patients sometimes combine bonding with other treatments later on, especially if they are considering
to improve the position of their teeth.
Composite veneers cover the front surface of a tooth to change its appearance. They can improve the shape, size, or visible colour of your teeth that stand out when you smile.
This option may be considered if you would like to address:
During your consultation, Dr Kamalie will look closely at your bite and surrounding teeth. In some situations,
such as fillings or crowns, may first be needed to support the health of the tooth.

Composite injection moulding is a technique used to shape composite material across several teeth with the help of a custom mould. This approach can help create more uniform shapes and smoother contours.
Patients sometimes explore this option when they want to refine the overall appearance of their smile. It can be especially helpful when small adjustments are needed across multiple teeth.
Maintaining your results still relies on good oral care. Regular check-ups and professional scale and polish appointments help keep your teeth clean and support the longevity of cosmetic treatments.
Is cosmetic dentistry right for me?
Cosmetic dentistry often begins with a simple conversation. You may want to refine the shape of a tooth, close a gap, or smooth edges that have been worn over time.
If you are looking for a dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein, you can
an appointment with Dr Kamalie to discuss your smile and explore cosmetic options that suit your goals.
Dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein | Orthodontics | Dr EA Kamalie Dental Surgery
Explore orthodontic care with a Dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein offering clear aligners, retainers, and treatments to improve tooth alignment. Book now.

Teeth do not always grow into place the way we expect. Some overlap, others drift slightly forward or sit at an angle. Over time, this can affect more than appearance. It can change how your bite feels and make certain areas harder to clean.

Orthodontic treatment focuses on guiding teeth into better positions so that your bite works comfortably and your smile feels balanced. Dr Emile Kamalie provides orthodontic care as a dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein, helping both teenagers and adults explore ways to improve tooth alignment.
Straight teeth are not only about appearance. Alignment also plays a role in how evenly pressure is distributed when you chew.
When teeth sit too closely together or overlap, cleaning certain areas becomes more difficult. This may increase the chance of plaque buildup over time. That is why
and scale and polish appointments remain important before and during orthodontic care.
Proper alignment can also make it easier to maintain long-term oral health.

Orthodontic care today often focuses on discreet solutions that fit into everyday life. One option offered is clear aligners, which gradually guide teeth into position through a series of custom trays.

Clear aligners are designed to be worn throughout the day and removed when eating and brushing. During your consultation, Dr Kamalie will assess your teeth and bite to determine whether this approach may be suitable for you.
Orthodontic treatment may help you address concerns such as:
Teeth that overlap or crowd together
Gaps that affect the appearance of your smile
Bites that feel uneven when chewing
Teeth that have shifted over time
Before starting orthodontic care, Dr Kamalie may check whether any
is needed first. In some cases, a filling or a crown may help stabilise a tooth before alignment begins.
Once teeth have moved into their new positions, maintaining that alignment becomes the next step. Teeth can slowly shift over time if they are not supported.
This is where retainers become important. Retainers are designed to hold teeth in place after orthodontic treatment so that the results remain stable.

Some patients also benefit from mouthguards, particularly if they participate in sports. A well-fitted mouthguard helps protect teeth from impact and can prevent injuries that may otherwise require treatments such as bonding or crowns.
How does orthodontics fit into my overall dental care?

Orthodontic treatment works best when it is part of a broader dental care plan. Healthy gums, well-maintained teeth, and routine visits help support the process. Many patients combine orthodontic treatment with other dental services over time. For example, some choose to refine their smile later with
such as composite bonding or veneers, once alignment has improved.
Is it time to explore orthodontic treatment?
If you have been thinking about straightening your teeth or improving your bite, an orthodontic consultation can help you understand the available options.
To learn more about orthodontic care with a dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein,
an appointment with Dr Emile Kamalie and discuss the next steps for your smile.
Dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein | Endodontics | Dr EA Kamalie Dental Surgery
Endodontic care with a Dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein offering root canal treatment to relieve tooth pain and help preserve natural teeth. Book now.

Tooth pain has a way of demanding your attention. Sometimes it begins as a mild sensitivity, other times it arrives suddenly and makes everyday things like eating or drinking uncomfortable. When pain develops deep inside a tooth, the cause may involve the inner tissue of the tooth. This is where endodontic treatment, or root canal therapy, becomes important.

Dr Emile Kamalie provides endodontic care as a dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein, helping patients address infections inside the tooth while preserving the natural tooth structure wherever possible.
Inside each tooth is a small chamber containing nerves and blood vessels. When this inner tissue becomes inflamed or infected, the tooth may become painful or sensitive.
Endodontic treatment focuses on removing the infected tissue, cleaning the inside of the tooth, and sealing it so that the tooth can continue to function.
You might need this type of treatment if you experience:
Persistent tooth pain that does not settle
Swelling around the gum near a specific tooth
Sensitivity to temperature that lingers after eating or drinking
During your appointment, Dr Kamalie may use
to examine the roots of the tooth and determine the extent of the infection.
How does root canal treatment help save a tooth?

When the infection reaches the centre of the tooth, removing the infected tissue can prevent the problem from spreading further. Once the inner chamber has been cleaned and sealed, the tooth remains in place and continues to function.
After root canal treatment, the tooth may sometimes require additional support. In these situations,
such as a crown, may be recommended to strengthen the tooth and protect it during everyday use.

Maintaining good oral health habits remains important after treatment. Routine dental visits and professional scale and polish appointments help monitor the treated tooth and support the health of surrounding teeth.
What can I expect during the procedure?
Many patients feel relieved to learn that endodontic treatment is usually performed with local anaesthesia to keep the area comfortable.
The infected tissue is removed from the root canals
The canals are cleaned and shaped
The tooth is sealed to prevent reinfection
In certain situations, especially for patients who feel anxious about dental procedures, conscious sedation may be considered to help create a calmer treatment experience.
Why treat the infection rather than remove the tooth?

Preserving your natural tooth can help maintain the stability of your bite. When a tooth is removed, nearby teeth may shift over time. This can sometimes lead to changes that later require restorative work or
to correct. Saving the tooth when possible allows you to continue chewing normally and maintain your natural smile.
When should I see a dentist about tooth pain?
Tooth pain rarely improves on its own. If you notice discomfort that persists or worsens, it is important to have the tooth examined.
If you are looking for a dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein, you can
an appointment with Dr Kamalie to assess the cause of your tooth pain and discuss whether endodontic treatment may be appropriate.
Dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein | Paediatric Dentistry | Dr EA Kamalie Dental Surgery
Paediatric dentistry with a Dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein providing gentle dental care for children and support for healthy, growing smiles. Book now.

Children experience dentistry differently from adults. For many, a dental visit is something completely new, from the sounds to the lights to the unfamiliar chair. A calm and welcoming environment can make a big difference in how children feel about looking after their teeth as they grow.

Dr Kamalie provides dental care for children as a dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein, helping young patients become comfortable with dental visits while supporting the healthy development of their teeth.
When should my child visit the dentist?
Children benefit from early dental visits. These appointments help monitor how teeth are developing and give your child time to become familiar with the dental environment.
During a visit, Dr Kamalie may look at:
may be recommended to examine areas that cannot be seen easily during a visual check. These images help identify concerns early and guide future treatment if needed.
Routine visits also create opportunities for gentle scale and polish cleanings, which help remove plaque and keep young teeth healthy.
What dental treatments might children need?
Children’s dental needs change as they grow. Baby teeth may develop cavities, become damaged during play, or occasionally need removal if they are affecting surrounding teeth.
Treating small areas of tooth decay with fillings
Removing a tooth that is causing discomfort or crowding
Monitoring how adult teeth are emerging

If a tooth becomes infected or severely damaged, treatment may sometimes involve care similar to what adults receive when the inner part of the tooth is affected. Addressing these concerns early helps prevent discomfort and supports normal development.
As children grow older, some families also begin to explore options that guide tooth alignment, particularly if teeth appear
What if my child feels anxious about dental treatment?
Dental anxiety can affect children just as it affects adults. Some children feel uneasy sitting still for treatment, especially during their first few visits.

In certain situations, conscious sedation may be considered to help children remain calm and relaxed during a procedure. This approach allows treatment to be completed more comfortably when a child is struggling to manage the experience.
For children who are active in sports,
can also help protect developing teeth from injuries during play.
How can I support my child’s oral health at home?
Dental care continues long after leaving the practice. Small daily habits can make a lasting difference.
You can support your child’s oral health by:
Encouraging regular brushing with fluoride toothpaste
Limiting frequent sugary snacks or drinks
Consistent care helps reduce the need for more complex treatments later and supports healthy teeth as your child grows.
Is it time to book my little one’s dental visit?
Helping your little one feel comfortable with dental care early can shape their relationship with oral health for years to come.
If you are looking for a dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein, you can
an appointment with Dr Kamalie to discuss your child’s dental needs and plan their next visit.
Dentist in Muizenberg and Strandfontein | Contact Us | Dr EA Kamalie Dental Surgery
